baseline.summary: RSSmaster is functional and well covered, but ownership is concentrated in frontend and workspace orchestration files.

auth.role: canonical domain owner for local accounts, sessions, cookies, and account-to-workspace resolution
next_refactor_tasks: Keep auth UI isolated from ChannelLab state growth.
Keep background jobs using explicit captured account workspace context.

reader.role: target frontend owner for queue browsing, article surface, triage, reader continuity, empty states, and reading quality
next_refactor_tasks: Extract reader state and route synchronization out of ChannelLab.
Move reader API calls and DTOs into a typed web API client.
Keep empty-state diagnostics covered by unit and browser checks.

sources.role: canonical owner for source onboarding, autodiscovery, OPML, source controls, sync entrypoints, and source health UX
next_refactor_tasks: Separate source discovery from source backoffice rendering in the web shell.
Make source controls use one canonical persistence model.
Keep preview/add/sync feedback calm and source-specific.

library.role: canonical domain owner for tags, collections, saved searches, and library retrieval surfaces
next_refactor_tasks: Choose one schema path for tags, collections, and saved searches.
Remove repository-level lazy table creation except compatibility shims.
Keep item state semantics aligned with reader and digest flows.

annotations.role: canonical owner for highlights, notes, annotation hub, and item-level knowledge capture
next_refactor_tasks: Move annotation mutations out of workspace facade.
Keep capture-created notes visible through the same annotation owner.

digest.role: canonical owner for persisted candidates, preview, build, history, delivery preflight, and delivery logs
next_refactor_tasks: Keep digest selection independent from visible reader filters.
Keep delivery dry-run and live-send responsibilities separate.

capture.role: owner for outside-app URL capture, note handoff, and saved-reader recovery
next_refactor_tasks: Move capture API semantics from workspace service into a capture owner or compatibility adapter.
Keep unauthenticated capture notice covered by browser smoke.

ranking_stories.role: canonical owner for ranking pipeline, story clusters, briefing recommendations, and discovery signals
next_refactor_tasks: Keep workspace endpoints as aggregation only.
Document which signals are ranking inputs versus presentation labels.

workspace_facade.role: compatibility facade and UI aggregation layer only; domain logic must delegate to canonical owners
next_refactor_tasks: Split workspace service into facade methods that delegate to library, annotations, ranking, stories, source management, and capture.
Keep /api/v1/workspace routes backward compatible until frontend call sites are migrated.

storage_migrations.role: canonical owner for SQLite schema, migrations, startup readiness, and recovery diagnostics
next_refactor_tasks: Keep all schema additions versioned and idempotent.
Retire duplicate storage paths only with explicit compatibility checks.

qa_harness.role: canonical owner for release gates, browser smoke checks, performance evidence, artifact schema, and stale artifact detection
next_refactor_tasks: Split check_api into route manifest validation and focused smoke sections.
Keep all browser checks writing the shared artifact schema.
Make ownership checks part of the fast unit gate.
